_comment: Issue #64 plan v2 Δ2: a vendored file using any deferred directive is skipped loudly and wholesale, with a concrete activation issue per blocking directive. Issue #154 made the block-expect forms (expect ordered, expect range vector) and load_with_nhcb EXECUTABLE — the only deferred directives left are eval_warn/eval_info (no pinned file uses them). The corpus test fails if a listed directive disappears from its file (activate the file!), if an unlisted deferred directive appears, or if a file is absent from both the executed set and this manifest. `manual_skip` (issue #124, M7-A6) is the non-directive lever: a file with ZERO deferred directives that still fails on replay for reasons outside any tracked directive — no drift check applies to it, unlike blocking_directives. Current users (issue #154 triage): aggregators.test (159/160 locally green; 1 row pending the #165 byte-escape ledger adjudication), functions.test (393/427; staged to #163 — 33 context-function rows blocked on #157 plus 1 #165 row). The classic-histogram corpus file was de-listed and activated by #164 (classic-bucket histogram_quantile lowest-bucket interpolation ported).

manual_skip.reason: 159/160 rows green after #154; line 481 (count_values("a\xc5z", version)) expects upstream's invalid-UTF-8 label-name failure, which the vendored parser's code-point \xHH decoding cannot reproduce — awaiting the owner-adjudicated ledger entry
393/427 rows green after #154; 33 start()/end()/step()/range() rows fail at parse (context functions, #157) and line 523 is the second \xHH byte-escape row (#165)
